message:
fix(storage): scope "parent blocked by open children" rollup to epic parents

Restricts the `load_local_open_child_blockers_impl` phase of the blocked-cache
rebuild so the synthetic `:child-open` blocker only fires when the parent side
of a `parent-child` dependency is itself an `Epic`.

## Why

With the previous unconditional rule, every `parent-child` edge made the parent
appear in `blocked_issues_cache` any time one of its children was still open —
even when the parent was an ordinary task and was itself already unblocked.  On
a transitive `blocker --blocks--> p0 --parent-child--> p1 --parent-child--> …`
chain this meant closing the root `blocker` could not actually unblock the
chain: each non-leaf still had an open child and so re-acquired a
`child:child-open` blocker in the next refresh.

Both `conformance.rs` and `tests/storage_blocked_cache.rs` document the intended
semantic:

> In br, children are blocked by the parent being open (parent-child creates a
> blocking dependency).

i.e., parents inherit their own direct blockers down the chain via
`propagate_blocked_parents`, and children unblock exactly when their parent
unblocks.  The reverse direction — "parent blocked by open children" — is an
**epic rollup** property (an epic cannot be meaningfully closed or worked on
until its children are done), not a property of every parent-child edge.

## What changed

`src/storage/sqlite.rs`:
- `load_local_open_child_blockers_impl` and `load_local_open_child_blockers_for_ids_impl`
  now join the parent issue and filter `p.issue_type = 'epic'`.
- Expanded comments document the epic-only scope and the tests that encode
  the opposite direction.

Tests:
- `tests/storage_blocked_cache.rs` now turns green for
  `parent_child_transitive_blocking`, `deep_parent_child_chain_blocking`, and
  `deep_chain_beyond_50_levels_blocks_all_descendants` — the three pre-existing
  failures that documented the desired semantic.
- `src/storage/sqlite.rs` unit tests `test_incremental_blocked_cache_update_recomputes_entire_parent_child_component`
  and `test_get_ready_issues_filters_by_parent` now explicitly type the
  parent(s) as `Epic`, which is what those tests actually want to exercise.
- `tests/storage_id_hash_parity.rs` fixes two pre-existing failures: `compute_id_hash`
  takes a SUFFIX of the base36 encoding (longer hashes end-with shorter, not
  start-with), and the Rust default prefix is `br-` (the `bd-` literal was
  from the Go prototype).
- `tests/storage_invariants.rs::list_filters_include_closed` now reflects the
  documented behaviour that `include_closed = true` still hides tombstones
  unless the caller explicitly requests them via a `statuses` filter, and
  adds that second assertion.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
